reports.text:
KRN heat exchanger Management interview Post Q4 2026 Earnings 

~Margin Expansion: Recent margin growth was driven by a higher share of data center billings (which carry better margins), solid export performance, and slight inventory gains

~FY27 Guidance: Margins are expected to improve by another 1% to 2% (targeting around 19% to 19.5%) in FY27, fueled by government incentives and an expanding export mix.

~Raw Material Inflation: Despite rising copper and aluminum prices, the company mitigates risk by utilizing pass-through price contracts with customers on a QoQ basis

~Capacity Expansion: KRN has expanded its capacity 6 fold compared to its old facility. The company aims for a 50% utilization rate this fiscal year.

~Exports: KRN is witnessing strong demand from North America, the UAE, and Europe. They expect to double their export revenue this year, starting with a record opening order book of around ₹120 crores 

~Sector Demand: While they have 0 revenue from room AC, they are experiencing significant demand in commercial HVAC segments, specifically from data centers and refrigeration 

~Bus & Railway HVAC: After hitting a milestone of supplying 500 bus AC units to OEMs, they are targeting a 15% market share in the bus system market this year. They are currently building a team to begin the 8-to-9-month vendor approval and development process for Indian Railways.

~Government Incentives (PLI & REIPS): KRN has already secured PLI approval, expecting to receive 5% of their finned tube type heat exchanger top line (approx. ₹20–25 crores) this year & They also anticipate Rajasthan's REIPS approval within the next 2 weeks, which will add a 1.5% benefit to their top line.

~KRN currently imports almost 100% of its key materials from Vietnam, Malaysia, Thailand, Korea, and China. They are developing domestic vendors to reduce inventory holding periods, though this shift will take 6 to 8 months

MS on IGL

EW. TP Rs 205

IGL's total volumes grew 6% YoY for quarter, while CNG volumes of 6% YoY; both lagged peers

Industrial and domestic PNG volumes grew ~4% YoY and ~14% YoY, respectively.

EBITDA margin came in at Rs4.8/scm, slightly below the mid-cycle average, as higher gas costs and currency impact erased benefits from lower pipeline tariffs.

IGL has raised gas prices Rs3/scm, which, per our estimates, passes on ~90% of increased costs on the back of depreciating rupee and higher commodity prices.

THE OFFSHORE SERVICES CYCLE IN INDIA MAY HAVE JUST ENTERED A NEW PHASE

The Under-Tracked Offshore Opportunity
- India’s largest fleet of multi-support vessels just delivered:
  - Highest-ever annual revenue  
  - Record profitability  
  - Sharp margin expansion  
  - Net cash balance sheet  
  - Expanding offshore fleet  
  - Rising deployment across deepwater projects  

- Yet, offshore oilfield service companies are still often viewed as:
  - Cyclical marine operators  
  - Asset-heavy low-multiple businesses  
  - Commodity-linked service providers  

- The deeper structural story may now be changing:
  - India’s offshore exploration cycle is gradually reviving after years of underinvestment  

1. FY26 Became the Breakout Year
- Consolidated FY26 performance reflected a sharp operating inflection:
  - Revenue crossed ₹1,000 crore  
  - EBITDA surged 83% YoY  
  - PAT nearly tripled YoY  
  - EBITDA margin expanded to 44.7%  

- Strong profitability expansion indicates:
  - Better vessel utilization  
  - Higher-value contract deployment  
  - Improved operating leverage  
  - Better offshore pricing environment  

- Cash profit at ₹411 crore highlights:
  - Strong internal cash generation  
  - Improving balance sheet flexibility  
  - Potential future fleet expansion capability  

- Standalone numbers also confirmed broad-based operational strength rather than one-off gains  

2. Margin Expansion Signals Industry Tightening
- EBITDA margin expansion of nearly 890 bps at consolidated level is significant because offshore service businesses historically operate with:
  - Volatile utilization  
  - High fixed costs  
  - Cyclical charter rates  

- Such margin expansion generally reflects:
  - Better vessel deployment efficiency  
  - Higher charter realizations  
  - Improved contract mix  
  - Stronger offshore demand environment  

- Lump-sum deployment of Seamec III materially supported profitability due to:
  - Better pricing visibility  
  - Improved operating economics  
  - Higher asset productivity  

- If offshore demand remains tight, operating leverage could continue to benefit margins over the medium term  

3. Offshore India Cycle Is Structurally Improving
- India’s offshore oilfield services market is projected to grow strongly through FY31 driven by:
  - Rising deepwater exploration  
  - Offshore reserve replacement needs  
  - Energy security priorities  
  - ONGC capex expansion  

- ONGC securing 7 of 10 blocks under OALP-VIII highlights:
  - Continued offshore exploration focus  
  - Long-duration development pipeline visibility  

- As onshore reserves mature, offshore development becomes increasingly important because:
  - Deepwater fields remain underdeveloped  
  - Domestic production needs structural support  
  - Energy import dependence remains high  

- This could create a multi-year demand cycle for:
  - Diving support vessels  
  - Maintenance support vessels  
  - Offshore installation support  
  - Subsea engineering services  

4. Fleet Expansion & Operational Scale
- Current operational fleet includes:
  - 6 Diving Support Vessels (DSVs)  
  - 1 Offshore Support Vessel (OSV)  
  - 1 Accommodation Barge  
  - 2 Handymax bulk carriers through Dubai subsidiaries  

- Acquisition-led expansion continues through:
  - Seamec Agastya integration  
  - Proposed acquisition of Seamec Anant from HAL Offshore  

- Fleet expansion matters because offshore service businesses are fundamentally:
  - Asset utilization-driven models  
  - Scale-sensitive operations  
  - Contract availability dependent  

- Larger fleets improve:
  - Tender participation capability  
  - Revenue diversification  
  - Geographic flexibility  
  - Customer engagement depth  

5. ONGC Relationship Strengthening
- Multiple operational developments reinforce strategic positioning with ONGC:
  - SEAMEC II contract extended through August 2026  
  - SEAMEC Diamond resumed ONGC operations  
  - Award received for maintenance services of ONGC-owned MSVs

- Long-duration ONGC engagement provides:
  - Revenue visibility  
  - Stable utilization  
  - Lower counterparty risk  
  - Strong domestic positioning  

- Consortium-based maintenance contracts may also deepen:
  - Technical service integration  
  - Long-term operational relevance  
  - Recurring offshore maintenance opportunities  

6. The Global Offshore Linkage
- Client profile includes major offshore and energy players such as:
  - ONGC  
  - ADNOC  
  - Saudi Aramco  
  - L&T Hydrocarbon  
  - POSH  
  - Mermaid Maritime  
  - Kreuz Subsea  

- International exposure matters because offshore cycles are increasingly global and linked to:
  - Crude oil economics  
  - Deepwater capex cycles  
  - Offshore infrastructure spending  
  - Energy security investments  

- Improvement in Dubai subsidiary performance suggests:
  - Better international vessel utilization  
  - Improved regional offshore demand  
  - Recovery in Middle East offshore activity  

7. Balance Sheet Has Turned Into a Strength
- Consolidated balance sheet now reflects:
  - Net cash position  
  - Negative net debt-to-equity  
  - Low leverage metrics  
  - Strong EBITDA coverage  

- This materially changes the financial profile because offshore companies historically faced:
  - High leverage risk  
  - Asset financing pressure  
  - Cyclical balance sheet stress  

- Strong balance sheet flexibility may support:
  - Additional vessel acquisitions  
  - Fleet modernization  
  - Strategic capex opportunities  
  - Better resilience during industry volatility  

8. Key Watch Item – Strait of Hormuz Risk
- Major near-term operational overhang remains:
  - Seamec Paladin stranded post dry dock in Dubai  
  - Awaiting safe navigation through Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia conflict  

- Risks from geopolitical disruption include:
  - Operational delays  
  - Temporary utilization loss  
  - Higher insurance costs  
  - Regional offshore uncertainty  

- However, this currently appears more like:
  - A temporary operational disruption  

- Rather than:
  - Structural demand destruction  

- The broader offshore demand environment remains constructive despite geopolitical volatility  

9. Industry Shift – Cyclical Marine Operator to Strategic Offshore Infrastructure
- Offshore service providers may increasingly evolve from:
  - Pure vessel operators  

- Toward:
  - Strategic offshore infrastructure execution partners  

- Competitive advantage may increasingly depend on:
  - Fleet availability  
  - Technical capabilities  
  - Long-standing PSU relationships  
  - Offshore execution reliability  
  - Multi-region operational capabilities  

- Structural industry tightening combined with rising offshore capex may gradually improve:
  - Asset utilization  
  - Pricing power  
  - Return ratios  
  - Earnings visibility  

Final Takeaway
- SEAMEC’s FY26 performance may indicate more than just a cyclical earnings spike. The broader story could be the early stages of a structural offshore services revival driven by deepwater exploration, ONGC capex acceleration, tightening vessel availability and rising offshore maintenance activity. Record profitability, sharp margin expansion, improving global operations and a net cash balance sheet suggest the company may now be entering a stronger phase of the offshore cycle. While geopolitical disruption around the Strait of Hormuz remains an important near-term watchpoint, the longer-term offshore infrastructure opportunity appears structurally intact.  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Offshore Energy Services Theme

reports.text:
Morgan Stanley on OMCs

Expect further price hikes in coming weeks.

What is the issue with Jain Resource Recycling results that came yesterday.

Revenue ₹9,543 Cr, up 48%.
PAT ₹352 Cr, up 59%.

Stock fell 17% today.

Let's understand why.

The growth is real:
Copper segment revenue ₹5,262 Cr, up 65%
Copper segment profit ₹194 Cr, up 85%
Lead segment revenue ₹3,818 Cr, up 36%
Lead remains the cash cow, copper is the growth engine

The number that broke the stock:
Net cash from operating activities for FY26 = negative ₹602 Cr.

The company reported ₹352 Cr of profit. The bank account shows a ₹602 Cr outflow from running the business.

Where the cash got stuck:

Trade receivables: ₹129 Cr → ₹476 Cr (+268%)
Inventory: ₹675 Cr → ₹1,477 Cr (+119%)
Both grew far faster than the 48% revenue growth

How the gap was funded:
Short-term borrowings jumped ₹916 Cr → ₹1,271 Cr (+39%)
Growth is now running on debt, not on operating cash

The peer test- Gravita India, same business:
Same scrap recycling- lead, copper, aluminium
FY26 revenue ₹4,265 Cr, PAT ₹378 Cr
Net debt-free after ₹1,000 Cr QIP
Funding new ₹160 Cr copper plant from internal cash
ROIC ~24%

Less revenue. More profit. Zero net debt. Same scrap.

The Key message:

This is not fraud. Copper revenue is genuinely growing 65%, the forward integration into anodes and cathodes is real, and management has been transparent about working capital cycle stretching.
But at 87x earnings and 27x book, the market was pricing perfect execution. The cash flow statement broke that assumption.

One KPI to watch next quarter:
Operating cash flow.
If it turns positive in Q1 FY27, the growth thesis holds.
If it stays negative, today's 17% drop is the first move, not the last.

Goldman Sachs On Urban Company

Target Price ₹140

Recommendation Neutral

Strong growth though elevated near-term investments

Core services continues to show robust growth, with one third of the portfolio at 8% EBITDA margin

InstaHelp is scaling rapidly but weighing on near-term profitability given upfront investments

Expects steady state margins for Native to get to 10% of NTV in 4-5 years

International segment margins to converge with India core services margin with a 1-2 year lag

Why is market so excited about Sakar Healthcare?

It is evolving from a conventional formulation player into a potentially differentiated oncology platform.

What stands out:

* EU-GMP approved oncology manufacturing facility

* Vertically integrated API → formulation → dossier → commercialization model

* 55 oncology molecules developed; 32 launch-ready dossiers

* 125 global filings with 12 marketing authorisations

* Tech transfer relationships with-> Accord, Intas, Torrent, Glenmark, Zydus, Emcure

* Oncology facility still at just ~30% utilisation, leaving room for operating leverage

Q4FY26 numbers indicate an inflection point:

• Revenue: 71 Cr (+42% YoY)
• EBITDA: 26 Cr (+67% YoY)
• PAT: 11 Cr (+91% YoY)
• EBITDA margin: 37% vs 26% in Q3

Importantly, revenue was only +1% QoQ, while EBITDA grew 41% QoQ, implying the quarter was driven by oncology mix improvement + operating leverage, not just topline growth.

Management suggests oncology contribution has scaled from ~21% in FY25 to ~38% in FY26, materially improving profitability given oncology’s structurally superior margin profile.

That said, valuation is no longer cheap.

At ~₹1735 Cr market cap, the stock trades at roughly:
• ~6.8x sales
• ~57x trailing earnings

So this is no longer a value rerating story it is an execution story.

Key risks:
– Approval ≠ commercialization
– Export revenues can be lumpy / tender-driven
– 37% EBITDA margin is unlikely to be the steady-state margin
– If regulated oncology exports don’t scale, valuation can de-rate quickly

Key KPI to track over the next 2–3 quarters: oncology export revenue conversion.

SAI LIFE SCIENCES — EVOLVING INTO A SCIENCE-LED GLOBAL CRDMO PLATFORM 🧬⚗️

- Sai Life Sciences has quietly built:
  - 34 commercial molecules  
  - Relationships with 19 of top 25 global pharma innovators  
  - ₹1,100–1,300 Cr science-led capex pipeline  
  - Integrated Discovery → Development → Commercial manufacturing capabilities  
  - AI-assisted scientific workflows  
  - Expansion into:
    - Peptides  
    - ADCs  
    - Oligonucleotides  

- Yet, market perception still often treats it as just another chemical outsourcing company  

The Real CRDMO Moat
- Advanced CRDMOs are not built only on low-cost manufacturing  
- Real moat comes from:
  - Lifecycle continuity  
  - Embedded pharma relationships  
  - Process familiarity  
  - Regulatory integration  
  - Scientific infrastructure built around molecules over years  

- This fundamentally improves:
  - Customer stickiness  
  - Commercialization economics  
  - Operating leverage  
  - Long-term scalability  

Business Model Transition
- Sai Life Sciences increasingly evolving from:
  - Transactional chemistry vendor  

- Into:
  - Deeply integrated scientific execution platform  

Why This Matters
- Once a molecule progresses through:
  - Discovery  
  - Development  
  - Clinical stages  
  - Commercial manufacturing  

- Switching vendors becomes operationally difficult and risky for pharma innovators  

- This creates:
  - Long-duration customer relationships  
  - Higher visibility revenues  
  - Better margin potential  
  - Stronger utilization stability  

Commercial Molecules = Structural Advantage
- Commercial molecule count matters because:
  - Revenue visibility improves  
  - Manufacturing scale rises  
  - Regulatory familiarity deepens  
  - Incremental margins generally expand  

- Commercial-stage molecules typically carry:
  - Higher stability  
  - Longer lifecycle revenues  
  - Better operating leverage  

Next-Generation Modalities
- Expansion into:
  - Peptides  
  - ADCs  
  - Oligonucleotides  

- Indicates move toward:
  - High-complexity science  
  - Advanced biologics ecosystem  
  - Precision therapeutics manufacturing  

- These segments generally have:
  - Higher entry barriers  
  - Lower competition  
  - Stronger long-term growth potential  

AI & Scientific Infrastructure
- AI-assisted workflows increasingly becoming important in:
  - Molecule optimization  
  - Process development  
  - Data analysis  
  - Scientific productivity  

- Long-term CRDMO differentiation may increasingly depend on:
  - Scientific execution speed  
  - Data infrastructure  
  - Integrated digital workflows  

Capex & Expansion
- ₹1,100–1,300 Cr capex focused on:
  - Scientific infrastructure  
  - Capacity expansion  
  - Advanced modalities  
  - Integrated capabilities  

- Important because CRDMO scaling depends not just on capacity, but on:
  - Regulatory readiness  
  - Technical capabilities  
  - Commercial execution consistency  

Structural Industry Tailwinds
- Global pharma continues outsourcing due to:
  - Cost optimization  
  - Faster development timelines  
  - Complex chemistry requirements  
  - Need for integrated partners  

- India increasingly benefiting from:
  - China+1 diversification  
  - Scientific talent pool  
  - Regulatory track record  
  - Manufacturing scale  

Key Monitorables
- Commercial molecule additions  
- Client concentration  
- Utilization ramp-up  
- Margin expansion trajectory  
- Success in peptides & ADC scale-up  
- Return ratios post capex cycle  
- Regulatory execution consistency  

Final Takeaway
- Sai Life Sciences increasingly appears to be positioning itself as a high-integration scientific CRDMO platform rather than a pure chemical outsourcing vendor. Deep pharma relationships, lifecycle continuity, advanced modality expansion and science-led infrastructure investments could potentially strengthen long-term scalability, customer stickiness and operating leverage over the coming years  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Structural Story

reports.text:
JPM on Amber Ent

Neutral, TP cut to Rs 7650

3 main takeaways from earnings call,

1) Amber’s revenue growth guidance for FY27 came in below expectations; Consumer Durables growth at 12-13% (vs JPMe at 16%) inline with industry and Electronics at 40% (vs JPMe at 59%)

2) There have been some delays in bare PCB projects as trial production at Ascent Circuits will start from Sep-Oct’26 (vs earlier guidance of 2QFY27) and at Korea Circuits it expects construction to begin from Jun’26 and trial production by 3QFY28 (vs earlier guidance of 1HFY28),

3) Amber guided for EBITDA margins to compress by 50-100bps at a consolidated level in FY27 due to a combination of an increase in raw material costs (copper clad laminates and gold) and an increase in minimum wages in Haryana and Noida

Cut revenues by 7-8% and margins by 50-120bps, led by cuts in both Consumer durables and Electronics business, which drives 12-36% EPS cuts over FY27-28E

reports.text:
ADANI ENTERPRISES : SETTLES OFAC SANCTIONS CASE WITH U.S. TREASURY 🇺🇸⚖️

• 🇺🇸 Adani Enterprises entered into settlement agreement with U.S. Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC)

• 💰 Company to pay settlement amount of $275 million (~₹2,300+ Cr)

• ⛽ Case relates to alleged violations of Iran-related sanctions linked to LPG shipments sourced through a Dubai-based trader

• 🚢 From Nov 2023 to Jun 2025, company purchased LPG shipments purportedly from Oman & Iraq, which OFAC alleged originated from Iran

• 🏦 OFAC stated around 32 U.S. dollar payments totaling ~$192.1 million were processed for these shipments

• 🤝 Settlement includes mitigation benefits due to cooperation with OFAC and additional compliance commitments by the company

• 📉 Penalty reduced from maximum statutory amount of ~$384.2 million to $275 million

• 🚫 Settlement executed without admitting allegations made by OFAC

• 🇺🇸 Report also indicates U.S. DOJ is close to dropping related criminal fraud charges, subject to developments

🔴 Impact: Negative (Large settlement amount and sanctions-related allegations may weigh on sentiment, though closure of the matter reduces prolonged legal uncertainty)

• 📊 No change in shareholding pattern of Timken India after amalgamation

🟢 Impact: Positive (Simplification of corporate structure and operational integration could improve efficiency, reduce costs and strengthen long-term business execution)

Products: Disconnectors & Drives for 362 kV Dead Tank Circuit Breakers - currently zero domestic capacity for these products

Entirely new product lines for both India & international export markets - this is not capacity expansion, it’s a greenfield entry into high-voltage equipment segments

First production expected by December 2026 (tentative), fully funded through internal accruals - no equity dilution, no debt. Signals management’s confidence in the multi-year order pipeline and India’s grid modernization mega-theme

⚠️Watch Points

🔴 Revenue dipped 4% QoQ (1,701 Cr → 1,637 Cr) - minor, but seasonal Q4 didn’t outpace Q3 on top line for first time

🔴 Hedge accounting losses of 245 Cr hit Other Comprehensive Income for FY26 - forex derivative drag is significant

So what actually caused the 4% QoQ revenue dip?

It’s simply the nature of project-based revenue recognition - milestone completions and billing cycles don’t follow a straight line quarter to quarter. 

Nothing structurally wrong. The PAT actually grew 21% QoQ despite the lower revenue, which confirms margin discipline held firm. 💪​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Apollo Micro Systems Ltd

The company summarized its overall FY26 achievements and also shared a detailed vision for 2036.

These two snippets clearly explain the company’s long-term vision, the steps they are taking to achieve massive targets, and their ambition to become a global OEM player for " MADE IN INDIA"

INDOCO REMEDIES : COMPLETES TRANSFER OF OPHTHALMIC BUSINESS TO SUNWAYS 👁️💊

• 🤝 Company completed transfer of its Ophthalmic Division to Sunways (India) Pvt Ltd

• 🌍 Transfer covers multiple international markets including Africa regions like Kenya, Tanzania, Zimbabwe, Namibia, Ivory Coast & others

• 📦 Transaction executed as a going concern on a slump sale basis

• 📅 Transfer completed on May 18, 2026 after fulfilment of all conditions precedent

• 🏥 Ophthalmic business includes operations in India and several overseas territories

• 📑 Move follows earlier approved Agreement to Transfer Business (ATB) announced in April 2026

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Positive (Business restructuring could improve focus and capital allocation, though impact depends on utilization of proceeds and future strategy)

• ⚖️ Petition was originally filed seeking quashing of order dated December 16, 1998 passed by Chief Judicial Magistrate

• 🔄 Court granted liberty to raise grounds before appropriate court or authority through order dated 07 May 2026

• 🏢 BCCL stated it will take appropriate legal steps as per law and maintains that the award is not implementable

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Legal proceedings continue through appropriate forums, with no immediate financial or operational impact indicated)

Financial Performance
- Q4 Revenue: ₹11,332 Cr (+2.9% YoY)  
- FY26 Revenue: ₹44,873 Cr (+3.0% YoY)  
- Q4 EBITDA: ₹4,889 Cr (+4.9% YoY)  
- EBITDA Margin improved to 43.1%  
- ARPU increased to ₹190 (+8.3% YoY), marking 19 consecutive quarters of growth  
- FY26 Cash EBITDA stood at ₹9,217 Cr  

Balance Sheet & AGR Relief
- Bank debt reduced to ₹726 Cr vs ₹2,326 Cr YoY  
- DoT finalized AGR dues at ₹64,046 Cr vs earlier ₹87,695 Cr  
- Repayment schedule extended till FY41 with lower payouts till FY35  
- Reported one-time exceptional gain of ₹55,622 Cr  
- Q4 Net Profit surged to ₹51,970 Cr due to accounting adjustment  

Network & Operations
- Subscriber base stabilized at 192.8 million  
- Positive net subscriber additions in February & March  
- Invested nearly ₹16,000 Cr capex over last six quarters  
- Added ~30,000 broadband towers  
- 5G launched in 80+ cities across 17 circles  
- Average data usage increased to 20.2 GB/month  
- Data traffic grew 30%+ YoY  

Enterprise Business
- Strong traction across:
  - BFSI  
  - Manufacturing  
  - Logistics  

- Continued growth in:
  - IoT solutions  
  - Enterprise connectivity  
  - Digital infrastructure services  

Funding & Capex
- Maintaining ₹45,000 Cr three-year capex plan  
- Capex intensity expected to rise from Q1FY27  
- Advanced discussions with SBI-led consortium for:
  - ₹25,000 Cr funded debt  
  - ₹10,000 Cr non-funded facility  

Future Outlook
- Targeting sustained subscriber additions  
- Aiming for double-digit revenue growth  
- Plans to triple EBITDA over next three years  
- Focus areas:
  - Premium customer acquisition  
  - Stronger 4G/5G mix  
  - Brand strengthening post AGR resolution  

Final Takeaway
- Vodafone Idea’s AGR relief materially improves balance sheet visibility and reduces long-term repayment pressure. Stabilizing subscriber trends, rising ARPU, accelerating 5G rollout and planned capex ramp-up indicate gradual operational recovery, though execution and funding remain key monitorables  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term, Execution Dependent

ASTRAL Q4 : STRONG MARKET SHARE GAINS & CPVC BACKWARD INTEGRATION BOOST 🚰📈

• 🚀 FY26 plumbing volume growth stood at 15.8%, exceeding double-digit guidance

VEERHEALTH CARE : RECEIVES ₹2.45 CR REPEAT ORDER FROM LEADING FMCG COMPANY 🧴🌿

• 📦 Received repeat order worth ₹2.45 Cr for supply of face care products

• ⏳ Order to be executed within 45 days

• 🏢 Buyer is a leading FMCG company operating in domestic & international personal and household care markets

• 🌎 Company also pursuing additional business from existing institutional client in USA along with new US clients

• 💰 Veerhealth expects FY27 revenue of ₹50-70 Cr with PAT margin of 7-10%

• 🚀 Management stated revenues may exceed guidance considering ongoing domestic & export order discussions

• 🇺🇸 Company highlighted USFDA plant clearance as a key differentiator among small-cap peers

• 🪥 Veerhealth specializes in manufacturing pure vegetarian toothpaste and ayurvedic products

• 🎯 Company targets ₹100 Cr revenue with 10% PAT margin over next 24 months

reports.text:
DECCAN GOLD MINES : DISCOVERS SIGNIFICANT NICKEL-COPPER-PGE MINERALISATION IN CHHATTISGARH ⛏️🟡

• 🪨 Maiden drilling campaign at Bhalukona confirms extensive Ni-Cu-PGE sulphide mineralisation across 700m target zone

• 📏 Initial drill hole intersected over 60m of mineralised gabbro with potentially economic sulphide zones

• 🧪 High-grade interval included 2.6m grading 1.01% nickel, 0.29% copper & 0.2 g/t palladium

• ⛏️ Over 1,200m of core drilling completed across 7 drill holes over ~1.3 km strike zone

• 🔬 Pentlandite, chalcopyrite & pyrrhotite sulphides identified, validating nickel-copper-PGE potential

• 🇮🇳 Project could potentially become India’s first Nickel-Copper-PGE mine

• 🚀 Company plans to accelerate drilling to establish mineable resource & fast-track mining lease applications

• 🌍 Bhalukona licence among India’s first Ni-Cu-PGE licences granted under critical minerals push

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major critical minerals discovery can significantly enhance Deccan Gold’s long-term resource potential and strategic relevance in India’s critical minerals ecosystem)

SANATHAN TEXTILES : EXPANSION, RENEWABLE ENERGY & TECHNICAL TEXTILES PUSH 🧵⚡

• 🏭 Punjab integrated polyester facility Phase-I ramp-up completed; Phase-II to raise polymerization capacity to 950 TPD

• 📈 Technical textiles expansion at Silvassa progressing; capacity to double from 9,000 MTPA to 18,000 MTPA

• 🧶 Planning greenfield cotton spinning project to strengthen integrated yarn platform

• 🌍 Signed long-term renewable energy pact with Serentica Renewables for ~70% captive power requirement at Punjab facility

• ☀️ Dedicated wind & solar plants expected to be commissioned in phases during FY27-FY28

• 💰 Company to infuse around ₹48 Cr equity in phased manner for renewable energy arrangement

• 📉 Renewable energy sourcing expected to materially reduce power costs and carbon footprint

• ⚠️ Company highlighted volatility in PTA, MEG & cotton prices due to Middle East geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
SANATHAN TEXTILES Q4 ; Management says Despite global trade uncertainties, tariff issues, FTAs, and geopolitical tensions in West Asia, the company delivered a resilient FY26 performance through disciplined execution and operational optimisation.

The Punjab facility saw strong customer acceptance and onboarding of new clients. 

Going forward, the company will focus on technical textile expansion at Silvassa, Punjab Phase-II expansion, cotton yarn expansion in Madhya Pradesh, and scaling integrated manufacturing operations.

DR REDDY’S — SEMAGLUTIDE & BIOSIMILARS STRATEGY UPDATE 💊📈

Semaglutide Opportunity
- Current scale at few million units due to industrywide capacity constraints  
- Targeting ~6–7 million units by end-2026  
- Existing manufacturing capacity around 12 million pens annually  
- Capacity expansion planned further  

- Management expects semaglutide opportunity to become multibillion-dollar market within 2–3 years as:
  - More markets open  
  - Volumes scale up  
  - Pricing normalises  

Key Competitive Advantages
- Early mover advantage across multiple markets  
- Scale benefits while competitors ramp up  
- Strong formulation & backward integration capabilities  
- Presence across 80+ global markets with direct presence in 60+ countries  
- Focus on:
  - New formulations  
  - Device innovation  
  - Oral versions  

Canada & Global Expansion
- Canada identified as first major Western market opening  
- Initial focus on:
  - Supplying market  
  - Patient onboarding  
  - Building physician relationships  

- Brazil also expected to become meaningful opportunity  

Semaglutide vs Tirzepatide
- Management believes both therapies will grow strongly  
- Generic semaglutide expected to gain share due to:
  - Lower pricing  
  - Better affordability  
  - Wider patient access  

Margin Outlook
- Initial margins expected to remain highly accretive  
- Over time margins may moderate with:
  - Higher competition  
  - Volume scaling  
  - Pricing pressure  

- Long-term economics expected to resemble:
  - Strong generic margins  
  - Higher scale benefits  

Biosimilars — Bigger Long-Term Bet
- Management expects biosimilars to potentially surpass generics in US by early 2030s  
- Biosimilars offer:
  - Lower pricing pressure  
  - More stable market share dynamics  
  - Payer-driven adoption  

- US business gradually shifting toward:
  - Biosimilars  
  - Consumer health  
  - Innovative medicines  

Global Diversification
- Reduced dependence on US since 2018  
- Key growth markets:
  - Brazil  
  - Germany  
  - Russia  
  - China  

- Oncology identified as major long-term focus area globally  

Final Takeaway
- Dr Reddy’s is transitioning from a traditional generics company toward a diversified global pharma platform built around semaglutide, biosimilars, oncology and innovative medicines. The biggest strategic shift is management’s belief that biosimilars could eventually become larger than generics in the US market, potentially reshaping the company’s long-term growth and margin profile  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Structural Shift

reports.text:
INDIA’S NEXT BIG ENERGY BOTTLENECK — LONG DURATION ENERGY STORAGE (LDES) ⚡️🔋

The Core Problem
- India added 178 GW renewable capacity in last 5 years  
- Clean energy now forms 51.5% of installed power capacity  
- But storage infrastructure remains critically inadequate  

Massive Storage Gap
- Installed battery storage: only 795 MWh  
- CEA FY27 requirement estimate: ~47 GWh  
- Gap remains ~46,200 MWh  

- Without storage:
  - Solar power cannot be used after sunset  
  - Wind intermittency remains unresolved  
  - Grid stability risks increase  

Government’s LDES Plan
- Government preparing VGF support scheme for LDES by FY28  
- Part of India Battery Storage Vision 2047  
- Ministries involved:
  - Power Ministry  
  - Heavy Industries Ministry  

- Possible support mechanisms:
  - Viability Gap Funding (VGF)  
  - Interest subvention support  
  - ISTS charge waivers  

What Is LDES?
- Regular lithium-ion BESS:
  - 1–4 hour storage  

- LDES:
  - 8+ hour storage capability  

Key LDES Technologies
- Pumped hydro storage  
- Flow batteries  
- Compressed Air Energy Storage (CAES)  
- Sensible heat storage  

Why LDES Is Important
- Heavy dependence on short-duration storage may:
  - Reduce energy security  
  - Increase grid costs  
  - Limit 100% renewable integration  

Why Adoption Has Been Slow
- High upfront costs  
- Lower commercial maturity vs lithium-ion  
- Limited large-scale deployment experience  

Policy Framework Under Development
- Technology-agnostic LDES definition  
- Storage targets in NEP & ESO framework  
- National deployment roadmap  
- Pilot projects across different grid conditions  

Structural Demand Driver
- India expected to add another ~100 GW renewable capacity by 2030  
- Storage becoming essential infrastructure for:
  - Renewable integration  
  - Peak balancing  
  - Grid reliability  
  - Energy security  

Potential Beneficiary Themes
- Battery storage ecosystem  
- Pumped hydro developers  
- Power equipment manufacturers  
- Grid infrastructure companies  
- Smart grid & inverter players  
- Renewable EPC companies  

Global Context
- Countries including:
  - UK  
  - Germany  
  - California  
  - New York  
  - Australia  

- Already moving toward LDES deployment to stabilise renewable-heavy grids  

Final Takeaway
- India’s renewable transition is increasingly becoming a storage infrastructure story rather than just a solar and wind addition story. The emerging LDES policy push highlights that future renewable growth will depend on solving long-duration storage economics, grid balancing and energy dispatch challenges at scale  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Structural Theme for Energy Storage & Grid Infrastructure Ecosystem

MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE STOCKS — HIGH GROWTH OUTLOOK 📈⚡️

50%+ Growth
- Zen Technologies — 50%+ growth for next 2 years  
- Airfloa Rail Technology — 50% growth for next 2 years  
- Supreme Power Equipment — 50% growth for next 2 years  

40%+ Growth
- Atlanta Electricals — 40%+ growth for next 2 years  
- Macfos Ltd — 40%+ growth for next 2–3 years  
- Namo eWaste — 40–50% growth for next 2 years  
- TARIL — 40%+ growth for next 3 years

30–35%+ Growth
- Deep Industries — 30–35% growth for FY27 & FY28

Power Ministry Developing National Tariff Redesign Framework; Fixed Costs Up to 56% of Discom ARR But Fixed Charges Only 9% of Revenue — ₹25 Trillion Needed for 100 GW Nuclear by 2047

The Tariff Problem — Structural Mismatch
Discom fixed costs (thermal generator payments + transmission): up to 56% of Annual Revenue Requirement (ARR)

Fixed charges currently recovered: only 9% of total revenue

Gap: 47 percentage points between cost structure and recovery mechanism

Result: discoms rely on variable energy charges to recover fixed costs → volume risk + stranded costs

AIDA to ministry: "This highlights a structural divergence between cost and tariff recovery mechanisms"

Why This Creates Cascading Problems
Discoms pay fixed capacity charges regardless of power drawn — recovering via variable sales creates liquidity crises during low-demand
 periods (cool summers, economic downturns)

High-paying industrial/residential consumers shifting to captive power or open access → reduce energy consumption but remain on grid → unrecovered "stranded" fixed costs

Hiding fixed costs in energy charges prevents cost-to-serve pricing — high-consumption users subsidise low-usage high-contract-demand consumers

Sharp increase in fixed charges risks: higher industrial electricity bills reducing competitiveness + burden on small/low-income/rural households

Proposed Solutions
Discoms proposing: progressively increase fixed cost recovery over next 5 years (by 2030)

Target: 25% fixed cost recovery for domestic and agriculture categories; 100% for industry, commercial, institutional

Standardised two-part tariffs across all states:
Low-tension consumers: ₹ per kilowatt per month
High-tension categories: ₹ per kilo volt ampere per month

Separate tariff categories with fixed, variable, and time-of-day (ToD) rates for net-metering consumers

Process — Where It Stands
CEA held discussions with AIDA and distribution utilities

Detailed recommendations being shared with Forum of Regulators for comments

Still in consultation phase — not yet implemented

Nuclear — ₹25 Trillion Investment Needed (TERI Report)
India's 100 GW nuclear capacity target by 2047

Investment required: ₹23–25 trillion

Current nuclear capacity: 8.8 GW

Projects under development: expected to raise to ~22 GW by 2030–32

Annual capacity additions needed after 2030–32: 4.5 GW per year — "scale and pace become critical"

TERI recommendations:
Project-specific Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) for capital pooling

De-risk projects to lower long-term system costs

Enable private sector participation

Build competitive domestic supply chain

Core Theme 
India's power sector is confronting two simultaneous structural crises — a tariff architecture where discoms recover only 9% of revenue through fixed charges despite 56% fixed cost structures (creating endemic liquidity risk), and a nuclear ambition requiring ₹25 trillion and 4.5 GW annual additions post-2030 from a base of 8.8 GW today; the tariff redesign and nuclear investment push are both essential infrastructure for India's energy transition, but both require difficult political decisions — raising fixed charges hits consumers and industry competitiveness, while nuclear at scale requires private sector participation in a historically state-monopolised sector.

Management is focused on sustainable growth, margin resilience, and long-term value creation, with optimism for demand trends across product categories.

The company aims to drive top-line growth, expecting gradual improvement in absolute margins and margin profile towards FY25 levels.

The room AC industry is expected to grow 15%-20%, commercial refrigeration upwards of 10%, and commercial air conditioning 12%-15% in the coming year.

Capital allocation prioritizes Segment A (UCP) for periodic investments in capacity and R&D, with plans to continue these investments in the current year.

Ongoing cost optimization programs, including sourcing efficiencies, design innovation, and localization, are strategic priorities aimed at protecting margins and improving profitability.

Management expects full-year FY27 order wins to exceed INR30,000 crores, with a top-line growth of over 15% and a 75 basis point expansion in consolidated PBT margins.

The PBT margin expansion is expected to be around 75 basis points at the standalone level, with the consolidated level being slightly better.

For FY27, the B&F, T&D, and Oil & Gas businesses are expected to grow at over 20%, while the Water and Railways businesses are not projected to grow.

CapEx for FY27 is budgeted at over INR800 crores, funded by internal cash flows, with a focus on B&F, T&D, international projects, and plant modernization.

Order inflows for FY27 are anticipated to be driven by T&D and B&F in the first six months, and Oil & Gas and Urban Infra in the last six months, with T&D order inflow specifically expected to be in the range of INR13,000 crores to INR15,000 crores.

April Industrial Production at 4.1% YoY vs Estimate of 5.5%

April House Price Index at -3.5% YoY vs Estimate of -3.5%

April Retail Sales at 0.2% YoY vs Estimate of2.2%

April Unemployment Rate at 5.25 vs Estimate of 5.5%

OPENING CUES – WEAK OPENING

- Oil rises as US-Iran talks remain stalled
- Persian Gulf infrastructure attacks reported
- US inflation hotter than expected
- Asian markets open weak
- Dollar strengthens on Fed hike bets
- Treasury yields move higher
- Rupee may open under pressure
- FIIs remain cash buyers for second day
- FII shorts unchanged at 88%

Impact: Global risk-off sentiment may pressure equities

Morgan Stanley on Tata Steel

Target Price ₹215

Recommendation Overweight

Good results; good outlook

Posted strong results across both domestic and overseas businesses

Near-term outlook remains promising, supported by higher prices in India and policy support in the UK and the EU

Management are focused on cost savings and downstream expansion

ICICI BANK – ICICI PRUDENTIAL UPDATE

- Prudential Plc acquiring 75% in Bharti Life
- May reduce ICICI Prudential Life stake below 10%
- Prudential may lose promoter classification
- ICICI Bank to retain majority ownership

Impact: Ownership structure may change due to regulations

ACC , AMBUJA CEMENT 

Karan Adani Says Cement expansion delayed due to execution hurdles; priority on completing ongoing projects

Citi On SAIL

Target Price ₹180

Earlier Target ₹135

Recommendation Sell

Q4 Strong

Think EBITDA/t may have peaked

Debt/EBITDA could rise

Morgan Stanley on Voltas

Target Price ₹1349

Earlier Target ₹1409

Recommendation Equal-weight

Voltas' brand moat, distribution depth and market leadership in RAC are structural strengths

Elevated competitive intensity and volatility in commodity costs could lead to just gradual margin recovery over FY27-28

NTPC, HCC, BHEL (+VE)

US nuclear industry delegation in India to explore opportunities

Citi On Vodafone Idea

Target Price ₹14

Recommendation Buy

Q4: Improving KPIs

AGR reset and promoter backing improves funding visibility

Equity infusion signals promoter confidence

Should catalyse closure of the long pending bank funding

Kotak Securities On Premier Energies

Target Price ₹900

Earlier Target ₹790

Recommendation Reduce

Steady execution, timely stabilization of cell capacity key

Capacity expansion on track; among India's largest integrated players

KSolare inverter acquisition did not materialize and BESS commissioning has been delayed, which was a negative

Remember this:

The world around is beautiful when the world within you is peaceful.

UK based Prudential to Acquire 75% Stake in Bharti Life for ₹3,500 Crore

First major foreign investment in the life insurance sector after the government raised the FDI limit to 100% from 74%

Deal Valuation At Rs 4600-5600cr

WHAT HAPPENS TO ICICI PRU LIFE?

Sentimentally Negative

Regulatory norms require Prudential to cut stake in ICICI Pru Life to under 10%.

Prudential will seek appropriate timeframe for divestment

Prudential's Health insurance ops expected to start in 2026, on receipt of approvals

Src-Exchange Filings

PRUDENTIAL TO ACQUIRE 75% STAKE IN BHARTI AXA LIFE

OTHER KEY TIE UPS IN INDIA:

Prudential plc (UK): Operates as ICICI Prudential Life Insurance in partnership with ICICI Bank.

AIA Group (Hong Kong): Operates as Tata AIA Life Insurance, partnering with the Indian conglomerate Tata Sons.

Sun Life Financial (Canada): Holds a major stake in Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance.

BNP Paribas Cardif (France): Partners with State Bank of India (SBI) to run SBI Life Insurance.

HSBC (UK): Partners with Canara Bank to form Canara HSBC Life Insurance.

Allianz SE (Germany): Holds exposure through Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance in a joint venture with Bajaj Finserv.

Mitsui Sumitomo Insurance (Japan): Partnered with Max India to establish Max Life Insurance.

Generali Group (Italy): Partnered with Central Bank of India to form Generali Central Life Insurance.

Aviva Group (UK): Operates in India as Aviva Life Insurance in a joint venture with Dabur Invest Corp.

MetLife (US): Operates PNB MetLife India Insurance, backed by the state-owned Punjab National Bank
Macquarie downgrades Kaynes Tech to 'Neutral' ratings with a target price of Rs 3,250.

POSITIVE

Tata Steel-Strong performance both domestic & international

BREAKING: India just restricted silver bar imports.

Silver bars under HS codes 71069221 and 71069229 — including 99.9%+ silver bars, have moved from Free to Restricted.

That difference is huge.

A duty hike only makes imports costly.

A restriction makes imports permission-based.

That means India is no longer just taxing silver demand. It is trying to control the physical flow of silver into the country.

Why does it matter?

• Importers lose free access
• Physical supply will tighten
• Domestic premiums can rise
• MCX silver can disconnect from global silver during stress 
• Industrial and bullion users may rush for inventory
• Real price discovery can shift from paper charts to physical availability

Silver is no longer being treated like a normal commodity.

It is being treated like a sensitive external-sector asset that challenges forex reserves of the central bank.

When a country that is already dependent on imported energy starts controlling monetary metal inflows, it usually means one thing:

Pressure is building somewhere in the currency, trade deficit, or dollar liquidity system.

You don't own enough silver.

Import of a bar containing 99.9% or more by weight of silver is restricted

The Import Policy of items covered under ITC HS Code 71069221 and 71069229 are revised from "Free" to "Restricted"

The Illusion of Plenty: Oil’s Floor is Thinning

Global inventories are in a freefall, with 280 million barrels already consumed to buffer the current conflict. Despite a headline 8.4 billion barrels, only 0.8 billion are realistically accessible.

The "Strait of Hormuz" closure is a systemic shock. OECD stocks could hit operational stress levels by June, falling toward a 6.8 billion barrel floor by September if demand destruction fails to scale.

Bottom Line: Inventory is the only shock absorber left, and it's running dry. Expect extreme price volatility as the market shifts from managed SPR releases to a forced, high-price rationing regime.

Standalone Revenue came at Rs 73.4bn (+14% YoY), EBITDA came in at Rs 10.7bn (+28% YoY), and EBITDA margin at 14.6% (+153bps YoY, -75bps QoQ).

Reported PAT at Rs 6.3bn (+242% YoY) was aided by a tax write-back.

Positive surprise was led by gross margin delivery coming ahead of expectations at 47.3% (+210bp y/y), which is a multi-quarter high besides lower-than-anticipated A&P spends (-5% y/y) and employee costs (+2% y/y).

Further, management narrative sounds positive regarding growth trajectory in FY27, noting a strong set up for FY27 enabled by progressive policy interventions in Karnataka (steep price reductions for P&A portfolio) and potential benefits from UK FTA

MORGAN STANLEY ON SAI LIFE SCIENCES

Target price: ₹1,160

FY26 EBITDA margin at 28.8% remains within management guidance

Brokerage projects ~30% EBITDA margin for FY27 and FY28

Net income rises 18% YoY to 1 bn in 4QFY26

**INDIA’S ELECTRICITY DISTRIBUTION CAPEX CYCLE MAY HAVE TURNED UP AGAIN ⚡️📈**

- Electricity distribution capex cycle showed strong revival signs in FY26 after nearly four years of contraction  
- Key indicators surged:
  - New project announcements ▲ 8.4x YoY  
  - Projects under implementation ▲ 4.6x YoY  
  - Outstanding project pipeline ▲ 3.5x YoY  

- Previous cycle peaked during FY16–FY20:
  - FY16 announcements reached ₹13,010 Cr across 13 projects  
  - Outstanding pipeline crossed ₹2 lakh Cr  
  - Projects under implementation peaked at 76 worth ₹13,330 Cr in FY20  

- By FY25, the cycle had almost collapsed:
  - Only 3 announcements worth ₹64 Cr  
  - 5 projects under implementation worth ₹144 Cr  
  - Outstanding pipeline reduced to 7 projects worth ₹197 Cr  
  - Project completions were zero in FY25  

- FY26 marked clear reversal with project completions resuming:
  - 3 completions vs 0 in FY25  

- Possible drivers behind the revival:
  - RDSS rollout acceleration  
  - Discom restructuring  
  - Renewable energy integration needs  
  - T&D infrastructure expansion  
  - Rising electricity demand  

- Distribution infrastructure historically remained weakest link in India’s power ecosystem due to:
  - High AT&C losses  
  - Weak discom finances  
  - Underinvestment in grid infrastructure  

- Sustained revival could benefit:
  - Power distribution EPC companies  
  - Cable & conductor manufacturers  
  - Transformer makers  
  - Smart metering ecosystem  
  - Grid automation players  
  - T&D infrastructure companies  

- Simultaneous increase in:
  - Project count  
  - Project value  
  - Execution activity  

strongly suggests a new capex cycle may be forming in India’s power distribution sector  

**Final Takeaway**
- India’s electricity distribution segment appears to be entering a fresh investment cycle driven by grid modernization, renewable integration and discom reforms. If execution sustains, distribution infrastructure could become one of the next major long-duration themes within the broader Indian power capex story  

**Impact**
- Positive Long-Term Structural Theme

Jefferies on HPCL
U-P, TP Rs 275

EBITDA was 63% above JEFe on higher refining driven by inventory gain.

Marketing margins remain sharply negative with losses on petrol/diesel.

Company faces headwinds with high physical crude premiums, higher freight costs and depreciating currency.

Cut FY27E PAT to –Rs 178bn and FY28E by 18%.

JPM on Kaynes
Downgrade to N, TP cut to Rs 4000

Missed its own 4QFY27 revenue guidance by 27% & even missed lowered Street/JPM expectations by 18%/13%.

Net working capital days remained elevated at 125 days vs. guidance of 85 days

Cutting earnings by 12-17% over the next two years led by cuts across core EMS, OSAT and PCB businesses

Cutting core EMS multiple to 33x from 45x led by

1) a cut in revenue growth over the next two years as well over the medium to long term in our DCF; and

2) an increase in NWC days over the medium term in our DCF.
While still expect strong 40%/45% revenue/earnings CAGR over FY26-28E thanks to the ramp up of OSAT and PCB businesses, we believe the stock will remain a ‘show me’ stock until the gap between actual numbers and company guidance narrows.

CLSA on Kanes
O-P, TP Rs 4200

Expect stock to react negatively to its 4Q results, which missed company guidance & street expectations on operational parameters

Its balance sheet also deteriorated further, which was a key item heading into its results.
4Q revenue grew 26% YoY to Rs12.4bn (CLSA: Rs15.7bn; company guidance: Rs17bn) while its margin fell 150bps YoY to 15.6% (CLSA: 16.5%)

Calculate working capital increased 85 days YoY (2Q: 116 days) while net debt was at Rs711mn (FY25 net debt: Rs1.9bn).

Weak IVECO, Higher fuel prices & commodity pressures near term risk for India CVs

CV segment: EBITDA margin at 13.2% (Nomura: 13.4%)

Given war-related challenges, risks are there to demand

Stock currently trades at 10.6x FY28F EV/EBITDA.

Nomura on HPCL

Downgrade to Neutral, TP Rs 440
4Q beats; near-term outlook negative

Staring at significant losses in 1QFY27F; LPG under-recovery of Rs680/cylinder highest ever

Expect sharp losses in fuel and LPG marketing segment in 1QFY27F with current run rate of Rs27/litre blended loss in petrol/diesel & Rs680/cylinder under recovery in LPG

Believe fuel retailing losses will be partly offset by a windfall tax on diesel exports for standalone refiners; however, OMCs are staring at significant losses in the absence of meaningful fuel price hikes.

GS on HPCL
Neutral, TP Rs 310

4Q EBITDA (Rs90bn) was significantly above GSe potentially driven by higher-than-expected inventory gains and lagging gains from lower cost crude from the previous quarter.

Key takeaways from management call

Management guided a loss in 1Q driven by higher crude oil prices,      sharp depreciation of INR against the USD, &  lack of inventory      gains

(2) Co maintained crude inventories at 2 months of refinery runs, unchanged from pre-war level. Crude sourcing from the Persian Gulf were mainly replaced by Russian barrels, while sourcing from West Africa, US and Venezuela also increased.

(3) Margin accretion from Vizag refinery's bottom upgradation unit is delayed by a quarter due to operational issues, now expected to accrue in 2Q.

CLSA on Crompton Cons
O-P, TP Raised to Rs 315

New initiatives are gradually taking shape, with solar pump revenues doubling, a growing orderbook in rooftops and a wider rollout of wires.

Given largely outsourced model, see these categories as low margin but high ROCE.

4QFY26 consolidated Ebitda of Rs2.7bn (+2% YoY) was slightly above estimate led by strong kitchen appliances (Butterfly, BGAL) and a turnaround in lighting growth.

Electrical consumer durables (ECD) growth was largely helped by BLDC fans, small domestic appliances and solar pumps
Raise FY27/28CL EPS 6-7%

HSBC on Crompton Cons
Buy, TP Rs 360

PBT before one-offs was flat y-o-y in 4Q; INR7bn goodwill impairment loss led to INR5.3bn net loss in quarter

Margin pressure to be tackled by pricing action, cost control measures amid FY27 demand challenges

Crompton trades at a 23x PE on FY28e consolidated EPS of Rs 12.26

JPM ON Cipla
Upgrade to OW, TP Rs 1550

Upgrade on improved earnings growth visibility driven by complex US launches over next two years, coupled with attractive valuations post the recent correction.

Management guided for a US exit run-rate of $1bn in FY27, a material step-up from the current ~$155mn quarterly run-rate
Pipeline looks promising with gVentolin (launch soon), gAdvair (1H), gSymbicort (2H), and select peptides including a material one
Keep estimates largely unchanged but raise the multiple as the pipeline now has more tangible milestones (approvals in hand, PAI done, goal dates), giving us higher confidence on execution. 

STK now trades at a 5-10% discount to peers like DRRD and Lupin on FY28E PER, offering a favourable entry point.

CITI on Cipla
Buy, TP Raised to Rs 1700

4Q trends were decent – a) c15% growth in India; b) US sales at US$155m, not much decline QoQ despite gRevlimid/Lanreotide phase out; c) QoQ gross margin expansion.

EMEU/API businesses have been impacted from ongoing geopolitical/logistic issues and may come back in the coming quarters.

Forward looking Indicators: Management commentary on India business (double digit growth), US Sales (US$1bn exit run rate) and pipeline visibility for FY27E was positive.

FY27E EBITDA margin guidance (18.5-20%) is baking in the impact of rising input costs, providing comfort on downside

Co also sounded comfortable for 20%+ EBITDA margin in FY28E, which is achievable if pipeline delivers on time.

CLSA on TVS Motors
O-P, TP Rs 3900

4QFY26 Ebitda margin came in at 13.1%, 40bps above estimate

TVSL expects high-single-digit 2W industry growth in FY27 and is confident of outperforming industry growth on the back of new launches and strong growth in scooters, including its e-2W portfolio.

In 1H, TVSL noted there would be commodity cost headwinds that could amount to 3-5% of revenue.

However, price hikes implemented so far should mitigate 35% of the cost inflation.
In addition, through operating leverage, further partial price hikes, product mix improvements and cost-reduction measures, TVSL expects to offset this impact.

CITI on Crompton Cons
Buy, TP Rs 400

Reported better-than-expected growth in Lighting (14% vs Citi est 10%) & Butterfly (17% vs Citi est 8%) while its core ECD business growth was broadly in-line with Citi est.

However, margin in Lighting and Butterfly was below Citi est while ECD margin was broadly in-line leading to 5% miss on EBITDA.

However, Adj PAT growth (excluding Rs7.2bn impairment of Butterfly) of 7% YoY was 2% ahead of Citi est led by lower tax rate (20.4%).

Near-term demand growth outlook is strong led by strong summer season, low base and tailwind from relatively new categories (solar rooftop, solar pumps, etc.).

However, margins risk remains key monitorable amid RM inflation.
